domain: Japanese kintsugi (golden joinery; broken ceramic repaired with urushi lacquer + gold powder; crack lines deliberately highlighted as gold seams; original substrate preserved; visible permanent record of damage)
specific_mechanism: Visible in-place repair with golden marker — boundary of modification deliberately exposed; original substrate preserved; subsequent users see the patch as part of the object's history.
llm_application: KINTSUGI-PATCH: an LLM model-editing protocol that, for any weight edit Δθ_Π applied at coordinates Π ⊂ θ, additionally (a) registers the edit in an APPEND-ONLY VISIBLE PATCH REGISTRY (records diff, timestamp, edit reason, cryptographic hash); (b) at INFERENCE TIME, traces the forward pass for parameter coordinates that were edited — a token output that depends on any registered patch is FLAGGED with a 'gold-seam' provenance trail; (c) the registry is EXPOSED to API consumers (so downstream apps know which parts of model are post-hoc edited); (d) supports REGISTRY ROLLBACK to undo a patch. Differs from standard model-editing (ROME/MEMIT/KE) by AUDIT-FIRST: edit's existence is a first-class invariant at inference time, not just training-time bookkeeping. Differs from safety-policy prefix patching by editing WEIGHTS in-place and surfacing the trace.
novelty_claim: ROME, MEMIT, KE perform weight edits without inference-time provenance exposure. SaLoRA / SLIM (R370) cryptographically bind safety LoRA without registry-style visibility. Patching LLM like Software (2511.08484) uses prefix-prepended patches but no inference-trace exposure. KINTSUGI-PATCH differs by (a) APPEND-ONLY VISIBLE REGISTRY, (b) INFERENCE-TIME PARAMETER-INVOLVEMENT FLAGGING, (c) API-EXPOSED PATCH HISTORY, (d) REGISTRY ROLLBACK. The audit-first design is novel.
content_words: audit-first inference logging on edit involvement

llm_side: audit-first inference logging on edit involvement
